review_title.title: Stigmergy and Self-Organizing Systems in Swarm Robotics: A Systematic Review

research_problem.summary: Although stigmergy and self-organization are recognized as fundamental principles of swarm robotics, there is no comprehensive systematic review integrating bibliometric, thematic, and epistemological analyses of the field.
research_gaps: Lack of a unified conceptual and taxonomic framework for stigmergy and self-organization in swarm robotics.
Limited understanding of the relationships between biological inspiration, coordination mechanisms, robot architectures, and distributed control strategies.
Insufficient discussion of practical challenges associated with transferring swarm intelligence methods from simulation environments to real robotic platforms.
Fragmentation of methodologies and absence of comprehensive analyses of emerging research trends and future research directions.

review_objectives.general_objective: To systematically organize, classify, and synthesize the scientific literature on stigmergy and self-organization in swarm robotics through bibliometric, thematic, and epistemological analyses.
specific_objectives: Define and distinguish the concepts of stigmergy and self-organization from the perspective of distributed robotic systems.
Identify and classify coordination mechanisms, distributed control strategies, architectures, and bio-inspired algorithms.
Develop a hierarchical thematic taxonomy representing the conceptual structure of the research field.
Characterize publication trends, geographical distribution, citation impact, and collaboration networks.
Identify emerging topics and temporal evolution of research themes.
Analyze representative application domains of stigmergic and self-organizing robotic systems.
Identify current research gaps, technical challenges, and promising future research directions.

research_questions: How have stigmergy and self-organization been conceptualized and applied in swarm robotics?
Which coordination mechanisms, architectures, and algorithms are most frequently adopted?
How is the scientific knowledge organized within the field?
What are the major publication trends, collaboration patterns, and geographical distributions?
Which research topics have recently emerged, and how have they evolved over time?
What challenges and future opportunities remain open for the development of swarm robotic systems?
boolean_query: ("stigmergy" OR "stigmergic" OR "indirect coordination" OR "environmental marking" OR "digital pheromone") AND ("self-organization" OR "self-organizing" OR "emergent behavior" OR "decentralized control" OR "collective behavior" OR "pattern formation") AND ("swarm robotics" OR "swarm robot*" OR "multi-robot system*" OR "multi-agent system*" OR "robot swarm*" OR "collective robotics")

inclusion: Studies addressing concepts, models, methodologies, architectures, algorithms, or applications related to stigmergy, self-organization, and swarm robotics.
Studies providing sufficient methodological and technical information for analysis.
exclusion: Conference papers, books, theses, editorials, and non-peer-reviewed publications.
Studies unrelated to the scope of swarm robotics, stigmergy, or self-organization.

software_and_tools: Large Language Models (LLMs) for supporting thematic organization and consistency checking

protocol_status.protocol: Prepared before the literature search and provided as Supplementary Material (Supplementary File S1).
protocol_status.amendments: Minor refinements to the analytical framework were introduced during the review process. These refinements did not modify the review objectives, search strategy, or eligibility criteria.
